summary: Phase A Squads 2-of-2 mainnet read-only wiring tested end-to-end. Backend (11/11 pytest pass) + Frontend (all testids + ordering verified). Real Helius mainnet RPC returns 0.001000 SOL for the configured vault PDA.

integration_issues.issue: Click on data-testid='squads-vault-pda-copy' did not produce a visible sonner toast in headless Playwright. Likely cause: navigator.clipboard.writeText is gated/silent under headless Chromium without explicit permissions context. Code has both success and error toast paths so the toast WILL appear in real browsers; this is an automation-context limitation, not a product bug. Worth a quick try/catch verification with a manual browser check.

action_items: (Optional) Manually click a copy button in a real browser to confirm sonner toast renders — automated check was inconclusive due to headless clipboard gating.
Phase B follow-up: implement the actual Phantom + Ledger 2-of-2 signing flow with typed-confirmation modal and 0.001 SOL test cap, as already promised in the squads-mainnet-warning copy.
critical_code_review_comments: services/squads_status.py: clean, async-correct, swallows RPC exceptions and logs them — degrades to rpc_ok:false instead of 5xx (verified by unit test test_get_squads_status_bad_rpc_returns_rpc_ok_false).

routes/treasury.py admin_dashboard: Squads block is wrapped in try/except so an RPC outage cannot 500 the whole dashboard — good. Defaults to {configured: False} on failure, matching the frontend's unconfigured-card branch.

SquadsVaultCard.tsx: 60s polling interval is sensible for a chain-balance widget. Refresh button correctly triggers immediate re-fetch. No memory leak — useEffect cleans up the interval. data-testid coverage is excellent (12 distinct testids).
TreasuryPanel.tsx: SquadsVaultCard is rendered FIRST (line 152), above HardwareSignerCard, in-app Phantom card, PerfSparkline, and SweepOldWalletCard — matches the spec.
Minor: TreasuryPanel.tsx Dashboard type still doesn't include `squads`; works at runtime but loses type safety. Low priority — strict TS would flag it.

Minor: services/squads_status.py is_configured() doesn't validate that the env-set RPC URL is well-formed; a bad URL still passes the gate and fails inside getBalance. That's the right design (graceful degradation), just worth noting.
seed_data_creation: None — used real on-chain Helius mainnet read against the configured vault PDA.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase A Squads (read-only mainnet) is verified green. Helius mainnet RPC works, vault holds rent-exempt 0.001 SOL. /api/admin/treasury/squads-status and /api/admin/treasury/dashboard.squads both return the expected payload. Frontend SquadsVaultCard is pinned above HardwareSignerCard. Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_squads_status.py for regression on any treasury-route changes. When Phase B (signing) lands, add tests for: typed-confirmation modal, 0.001 SOL cap, Phantom + Ledger 2-of-2 flow, post-sign vault balance delta.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs to RCA. The sonner-toast-after-copy automation gap is a headless-Chromium clipboard limitation, not a code defect.
